61:3.arabic: كَبُرَ مَقْتًا عِندَ ٱللَّهِ أَن تَقُولُوا۟ مَا لَا تَفْعَلُونَ
61:3.english: Greatly hateful in the sight of Allah that you say what you do not do.
61:3.score_notes: Base verb form. Saying and not doing — deeply hated by Allah.

lesson_use.heading: 6 · كَبُرَ (was great)
lesson_use.arabic: **كَبُرَ** مَقْتًا عِندَ اللَّهِ أَن تَقُولُوا مَا لَا تَفْعَلُونَ
lesson_use.english: **Greatly** hateful in the sight of Allah — that you say what you do not do
lesson_use.notes: This is the root word itself — **كَبُرَ** — in its simplest form. You can hear the three root letters clearly: kāf, bā, rā.
